# Annotator Informed Consent Form (Template)

**Project:** Pakistan-Focused Population Opinion Simulator for Company PR Scenario Forecasting  
**Version:** 1.0 · **Date:** 2026-06-25

> Adapt to your university's required consent format before recruiting annotators.

---

## Purpose

You are invited to label public text (news and social media excerpts) for sentiment, stance toward companies, emotions, and event types. Labels will train machine-learning models used in academic research on **aggregate** public-reaction forecasting in Pakistan's telecom sector.

---

## What You Will Do

- Read short text excerpts in English (and optionally Urdu/Roman Urdu in later phases).
- Assign labels according to written guidelines.
- Estimated time: 20–40 hours total, in sessions you schedule.
- You may skip any item that is offensive or distressing without penalty.

---

## Risks

You may encounter offensive or political content in public posts. You are not required to label graphic or abusive material. Contact `[researcher email]` if you need to debrief or pause.

---

## Benefits

No direct personal benefit. Contribution supports academic research and may be acknowledged in thesis acknowledgments unless you prefer anonymity.

---

## Confidentiality

Your identity as an annotator will not be published. Label files are stored securely and used only for this project.

---

## Voluntary Participation

Participation is voluntary. You may withdraw at any time without affecting your academic standing or relationship with the research team.

---

## Compensation

`[Specify: unpaid volunteer / honorarium amount / course credit / none]`

---

## Consent

- [ ] I have read this form and had the opportunity to ask questions.
- [ ] I agree to participate in annotation for this project.
- [ ] I understand I may withdraw at any time.
